questions.q: An atom of an element has mass number 23 and atomic number 11. What is its electronic configuration?
Which of the following is an isotope of the element with atomic number 8?
What is the total number of electrons and protons in an atom of an element with atomic number 19?
Fill in the blank: The mass number of an atom is the sum of the number of protons and _______ in its nucleus.
State the difference between isotopes and isobars of an element.
questions.answer: Isotopes have the same atomic number but different mass numbers. Isobars have the same mass number but different atomic numbers.

questions.q: What is the name of the scientist who discovered the nucleus of an atom?
Which of the following represents the correct order of electron energy in an atom?
What is the maximum number of electrons that can be present in an orbital?
True or False: Isotopes of an element have the same physical properties.
Describe the structure of an atom, including the nucleus and electron clouds.
questions.answer: The nucleus is the central part of an atom, consisting of protons and neutrons. Electron clouds are the regions around the nucleus where electrons are present. The electron clouds are divided into shells and subshells.
